revision.text:
{{Short description|Group of burial mounds in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asakita-ku, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of [[Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita Ward]] in the city of [[Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 burial mounds located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. [[Grave goods]] found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif [[bronze mirror]]s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[pit dwelling]]s from the [[Yayoi period]], which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, [[Hiroshima University]] and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q104093006]] Wikidata name: Nakaoda Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita Ward]] in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, [[:en:Hiroshima University|Hiroshima University]] and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q104093006]] Wikidata name: Nakaoda Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita Ward]] in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, [[:en:Hiroshima University|Hiroshima University]] and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita Ward]] in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, [[:en:Hiroshima University|Hiroshima University]] and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q104093006]] Wikidata name: Nakaoda Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of [[:en:Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|Asakita Ward]] in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, [[:en:Hiroshima University|Hiroshima University]] and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q104093006]] Wikidata name: Nakaoda Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita Ward}} in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, {{ill|Hiroshima University|en|Hiroshima University|WD=Q115220|lt=Hiroshima University}} and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|WD=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita Ward}} in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, {{ill|Hiroshima University|en|Hiroshima University|WD=Q115220|lt=Hiroshima University}} and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|WD=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|1=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q63444|lt=Asakita Ward|1=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}} in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, {{ill|Hiroshima University|en|Hiroshima University|WD=Q115220|lt=Hiroshima University|1=Hiroshima University}} and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|WD=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakaoda Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中小田古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Asakita-ku, Hiroshima, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoda Kofun Cluster | native_name =中小田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Nakaoda Kofun Clusterer | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.44741666666667|N|132.4948611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|qid=Q63444|lt=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|1=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|50.7|N|132|29|41.5|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nakaoda Kofun Cluster'''|中小田古墳群| Nakaoda kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kuchida-minami neighborhood of {{ill|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asakita-ku, Hiroshima|qid=Q63444|lt=Asakita Ward|1=Asakita-ku, Hiroshima}} in the city of [[:en:Hiroshima, Hiroshima | Hiroshima]], in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1996.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139284|title=中小田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakaoda Kofun cluster is a group of 12 Kofuns located on a hilly ridge at an elevation of 60 to 130 meters that spreads from south to north along the left bank of the Ota River. Most of them are round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', but one {{nihongo|''hotategata kofun''|帆立型古墳||}}-scallop-shaped mound, and one keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|}} have also been confirmed. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1979 of the keyhole-shaped tumulus, confirming a total length of about 30 meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found included triangular-rimmed "divine beast" motif {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, fragments of armor, iron weapons, beads and other artifacts which date the tomb to the latter half of the early Kofun period (the latter half of the 4th century). Excavation of one of the round tumuli recovered a bronze mirror, a helmet with a hammer, iron swords, (including a ceremonial a meandering blade sword) and other items from the first half of the middle Kofun period (first half of the 5th century). The foundations of three [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, which predate the construction of the tumuli by several centuries, were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The excavated artifacts are stored at the Archeology Laboratory of the Faculty of Letters, {{ill|Hiroshima University|en|Hiroshima University|qid=Q115220|lt=Hiroshima University|1=Hiroshima University}} and the Hiroshima City Board of Education.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|qid=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120200.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki/46/11736.html Hiroshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
